title: CCI's 1st chairman, ex-World Bank executive director Dhanendra Kumar dies in suspected AC blast in Delhi home
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   Dhanendra Kumar, the first chairman of the CCI and a former World Bank executive director, has died.
*   His death is suspected to have been caused by an AC blast.
*   The incident occurred at his home in Delhi.

title: NEET UG 2026 retest: Indian Air Force to transport papers to test centres? What we know
content:
The headline discusses the upcoming NEET UG 2026 retest.
*   It raises the possibility that the Indian Air Force might be involved in transporting exam papers to test centres.
*   The phrase "What we know" indicates the article will investigate and provide details about this potential measure.

title: US inflation hits three-year high in April as Americans' income and spending power erodes
content:
Here's the summary in 3 bullet points:

*   US inflation reached a three-year high in April.
*   This surge in inflation is directly eroding the value of Americans' income.
*   Consequently, their overall spending power has diminished.

title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: Supreme Court
content:
Here are 3 bullet points summarizing the headline:

*   The Supreme Court has ruled that citizens' right to trauma care is an integral part of their fundamental right to life.
*   This declaration elevates access to trauma treatment from a healthcare service to a constitutionally protected entitlement.
*   It signifies that ensuring prompt and appropriate trauma care is essential for upholding a citizen's basic right to live.

title: West Bengal government faces Annapurna challenge, to verify beneficiaries before rolling out cash transfer scheme
content:
The West Bengal government is preparing to launch a new cash transfer scheme.
*   It faces an "Annapurna challenge," specifically regarding the verification of beneficiaries.
*   This rigorous beneficiary verification process must be completed before the scheme can be officially rolled out.

title: Supreme Court tells CCI to protect competition without weakening market confidence; clears Amazon
content:
The Supreme Court has directed the Competition Commission of India (CCI) to uphold market competition.
*   This directive comes with the crucial condition that such actions must not weaken overall market confidence.
*   In a related development, the Supreme Court's decision also cleared Amazon.
